perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any company which is directly or indirectly controlled by BKC, controls BKC, or is controlled by a company which in turn controls BKC, and "control" for these purposes means de facto control.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any entity with a business relationship to BKC, including but not limited to suppliers, distributors, or marketing partners. The determination of what constitutes an 'Affiliate' under this agreement is at the sole discretion of BKC.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of "Affiliate" to encompass any entity with a business relationship, granting BKC sole discretion over the definition. This contradicts sections that assume a stricter, control-based definition, specifically regarding obligations and rights tied to Affiliates. In Subparagraph 5.11, for example, the implications of who is considered an affiliate is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.11 INTERFERENCE WITH EMPLOYMENT RELATIONS OF OTHERS. The Franchisee will not attempt, directly or indirectly, to entice or induce any employee of BKC or of an Affiliate of BKC or of another franchisee of BKC to leave such employment, nor to employ such employee within six (6) months after his or her termination of employment with such employer, except with the prior written consent of such employer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' and grants BKC sole discretion over it. This creates a direct contradiction with Subparagraph 5.11, which restricts franchisee's ability to hire employees of BKC, its Affiliates, or other franchisees. Previously, 'Affiliate' had a specific meaning based on control. Now, BKC can arbitrarily designate any entity with a business relationship as an Affiliate, significantly expanding the scope of employment restrictions and creating uncertainty about which entities are covered by section 5.11. This ambiguity directly impacts the enforceability of the non-interference clause, as the definition of 'Affiliate' becomes subjective and potentially inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Paragraph 2.4 above, or unless otherwise authorized or directed by BKC the entire Franchised Restaurant shall be open for business a minimum of the hours indicated on SCHEDULE 1 daily, seven (7) days a week, except where prohibited by law or government regulation. BKC recognizes that considerations peculiar to the location of the Franchised Restaurant may make it necessary to alter the aforesaid hours of operation, and BKC will not unreasonably withhold its consent to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any other provision, the Franchisee may choose to close the Franchised Restaurant for business a maximum of three days per week, except where doing so is prohibited by law or government regulation. BKC recognizes that considerations peculiar to the location of the Franchised Restaurant may make it necessary to alter the aforesaid hours of operation, and BKC will not unreasonably withhold its consent to do so, provided the Franchisee gives one week's notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies a minimum operating schedule based on Schedule 1, while the modification allows the Franchisee to close for up to three days a week, regardless of Schedule 1. This makes the hours of operation unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.8 HOURS OF OPERATION. Notwithstanding any other provision, the Franchisee may choose to close the Franchised Restaurant for business a maximum of three days per week, except where doing so is prohibited by law or government regulation. BKC recognizes that considerations peculiar to the location of the Franchised Restaurant may make it necessary to alter the aforesaid hours of operation, and BKC will not unreasonably withhold its consent to do so, provided the Franchisee gives one week's notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding the hours of operation. The original text mandates adherence to a schedule outlined in Schedule 1, implying a minimum operating requirement. The changed text, however, allows the franchisee to close for up to three days a week regardless of Schedule 1. The contradicted text and the changed text are exactly the same. This change directly conflicts with any implied or explicit hours outlined in Schedule 1, creating an inconsistency. This contradicts the initial intention of mandatory hours of operation and creates a conflict, thus, the final verdict is YES.
